content.text: CopilotKit is an open-source toolkit for embedding AI copilots in React applications. It provides a React provider, hooks for sharing app state and registering actions, UI components (sidebar, chat), and a backend runtime that connects to Claude. Instead of building custom streaming, tool routing, and chat UI from scratch, CopilotKit compresses that work into three primitives:

attrs.alt: CopilotKit architecture: React hooks share state and register actions with CopilotRuntime, which routes requests to Claude and streams AG-UI events back

content.text:
# Install frontend packages
npm install @copilotkit/react-core @copilotkit/react-ui

# Install backend runtime
pip install copilotkit anthropic fastapi uvicorn
Wrap the app (or a subtree) in
with the URL of the backend runtime. Nest the
inside so every component in the tree can register hooks.

injects a snapshot of React state into the model's context on every request. The model can then reference live data — tasks, user preferences, selected items — without you building a separate query endpoint.
The description field becomes a label in the system prompt: 'The user's current task list: [...]'
Multiple calls to useCopilotReadable are additive — each adds a labelled section
The value is serialised to JSON; keep state objects flat when possible
State updates flow to the model automatically on the next request
registers a named tool the model can invoke. The optional
prop is a React component that renders inside the chat when the action runs — this is CopilotKit's generative UI hook.
The handler receives typed, validated args from the model and returns a string result
render({ status, args }) is called with status 'inProgress' while the action runs and 'complete' after
The model receives the handler return value as the tool result in the next turn
Actions defined in React components are automatically registered/unregistered on mount/unmount

runtime handles the AG-UI SSE stream, routes tool calls to handlers, and injects readable state into the model's context. It can also register server-side actions (database writes, external API calls) that should not run in the browser.

# server.py — CopilotKit runtime with FastAPI
from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse
from copilotkit.integrations.fastapi import add_fastapi_endpoint
from copilotkit import CopilotKitSDK, Action, LangGraphAgent
import anthropic

app = FastAPI()

# Define server-side actions (optional — supplements frontend actions)
sdk = CopilotKitSDK(
    # You can register backend-only actions here:
    actions=[
        Action(
            name="fetchWeather",
            description="Fetch the current weather for a location",
            parameters=[
                {"name": "location", "type": "string", "description": "City name"},
            ],
            handler=lambda location: {"temp": 22, "condition": "Sunny", "location": location},
        ),
    ],
)

add_fastapi_endpoint(app, sdk, "/api/copilot")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import uvicorn
    uvicorn.run(app, host="0.0.0.0", port=8000)
Generative UI via the render Prop

prop. The model calls an action to display data; the render component shows it as a rich card rather than prose text. The render prop receives the streaming args in real time, so the card can animate from skeleton to complete as the model fills in the fields.
accept several props for tailoring the experience without building a custom component:
instructions: System prompt appended by CopilotKit to every request
labels.title / labels.initial: Header text and opening message
className: Tailwind class overrides for the outer container
icons: Replace the default chat bubble icon
CopilotKit reduces the work of adding an AI copilot to a React application from weeks to hours. The three core hooks —
, and the UI components — map directly to what a copilot needs: awareness of app state, the ability to mutate it, and a surface for the user to interact with. The

gives generative UI out of the box, with the model's streaming tool arguments flowing directly into a live React component. For production use, add server-side action validation, keep readable values concise to avoid bloating the context, and colocate each
with the component it belongs to.
